channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Central Myanmar shaken by new quake in one of the biggest aftershocks since March 28 temblor
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A magnitude 5.5 earthquake struck on Sunday morning near Meiktila, a small city in central Myanmar, according to the U.S. Geological Survey.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Western Michigan wins its 1st Frozen Four hockey title, beating Boston University 6-2
item.description: ST. LOUIS (AP) — Owen Michaels didn’t hesitate after watching his shot slide into the open net with more than two minutes left to essentially seal Western Michigan’s first Frozen Four title in its first championship game.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Wisconsin teen charged in shooting death of mother, stepfather found decomposed in their home
item.description: A 17-year-old Wisconsin boy is accused of killing his parents and leaving their bodies to rot in their home for weeks before fleeing the state in their SUV.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US won't say whether it's facilitating return of mistakenly deported man, despite judge's order
item.description: The Trump administration confirmed to a federal judge Saturday that a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported last month remains confined in a notorious prison in El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration says it will exclude some electronics from reciprocal tariffs
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — The Trump administration late Friday said it would exclude electronics like smartphones and laptops from reciprocal tariffs, a move that could help keep the prices down for popular consumer electronics that aren’t usually made in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MAE ANDERSON and MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Intellectually disabled teen shot by Idaho police dies after being removed from life support
item.description: Relatives say an autistic and nonverbal teenage boy who was shot by Idaho police from the other side of a chain-link fence while holding a knife has died.
item.dc:creator: Martha Bellisle And Rebecca Boone, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Regulators order corrective action as Keystone Pipeline operators aim to restore service
item.description: Federal regulators have ordered the operator of the Keystone Pipeline to take several corrective actions after a rupture caused 147,000 gallons of oil to spill onto farmland in North Dakota.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Kansas man who was falsely accused of being a shooter after Chiefs’ 2024 Super Bowl win has died
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — A Kansas man who was falsely accused of being among the shooters who opened fire at a rally celebrating the Kansas City Chiefs’ Super Bowl victory last year has died, his attorney said. Denton Loudermill Jr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Popular soup, bowl products may be contaminated; USDA issues public health alert
item.description: The USDA has issued a nationwide public health alert warning consumers that some popular soup and bowl products could be contaminated with wood.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, WGN 9

item.title: Pet food recalled after testing positive for salmonella and listeria: FDA
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has announced a voluntary recall of certain puppy and kitten foods.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US may already be in recession, says BlackRock CEO
item.description: BlackRock CEO Larry Fink said during a recent interview that the United States may already be in a recession or getting very close to one as a result of President Trump’s sweeping tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KTLA 5

item.title: Texas man suing NFL player over December shooting at car lot in Nashville
item.description: A Texas man who says Titans cornerback L'Jarius Sneed or his personal assistant shot at him while he was sitting inside a Mercedes-Benz on a dealer lot in December is suing for at least $1 million.
item.dc:creator: Teresa M. Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lawyers for Sean 'Diddy' Combs want jurors at trial questioned about sex, drugs and violence
item.description: Lawyers for Sean “Diddy” Combs want a judge to let prospective jurors at his upcoming sex trafficking trial be questioned about sex, drugs and violence.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump exempts phones, computers, other electronics from reciprocal tariffs
item.description: The Trump administration is exempting electronics such as phones, computers and other electronics from the "reciprocal" tariffs imposed on other nations, including China.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Take a trip to Ohio to learn about William McKinley, Trump's much-admired Gilded Age president
item.description: If there’s one place where interest in William McKinley has remained unwavering, it's in the 25th president's native northeast Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Julie Carr Smyth, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Strict Idaho abortion ban loosened by judge's ruling on medical exemptions
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — An abortion in Idaho is not prohibited if pregnancy complications could cause a woman's death, even if that death “is neither imminent nor assured,” a state judge said Friday in a ruling that loosens one of the strictest abortion bans in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge rules Menendez brothers’ bid for freedom through resentencing can continue
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Erik and Lyle Menendez’s resentencing hearings can continue despite opposition from the Los Angeles County district attorney, a judge ruled Friday.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Navy deploys another Houthi-fighting warship to new US southern border mission
item.description: The Navy on Friday deployed another warship to the southern border. The USS Stockdale will join at least two other Navy warships deployed in March.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Columbia University activist Mahmoud Khalil can be deported, immigration judge rules
item.description: JENA, La. (AP) — Columbia University graduate student Mahmoud Khalil can be forced out of the country as a national security risk, an immigration judge in Louisiana ruled Friday after lawyers argued the legality of deporting the activist who participated in pro-Palestinian demonstrations.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: South Carolina executes second man by firing squad in 5 weeks
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A firing squad on Friday executed a South Carolina man who killed an off-duty police officer, the second time the rare execution method has been used by the state in the past five weeks.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump wants Congress to end the changing of clocks and keep the country on daylight saving time
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Friday urged Congress to “push hard for more Daylight at the end of a day” in his latest dig at the semiannual changing of clocks.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon CEO: Sellers will try to pass tariff costs along to buyers
item.description: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy predicted that the retailer’s web of third-party sellers will try to pass the extra costs incurred by the Trump administration's wide-ranging tariffs on to buyers.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Plane crash near Florida highway kills 3 on board, injures 1 on the ground
item.description: A spokesperson for the Federal Aviation Administration said the plane, a Cessna 310, crashed at around 10:20 a.m. after departing from the nearby airport.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Walmart employee accused of killing co-worker inside store
item.description: A Walmart employee shot and killed a co-worker inside a Georgia store and critically wounded another worker outside early Friday while the business was closed to the public, authorities said. Newton County deputies responded to the shooting at the Walmart in Covington, Georgia, at around 1:30 a.m.
item.dc:creator: D.V. Wise, KTLA 5

item.title: NYC sightseeing helicopter plunges into river, killing 6 including Spanish family and pilot
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A New York City sightseeing helicopter broke apart in midair Thursday and crashed upside-down into the Hudson River, killing the pilot and a family of five Spanish tourists in the latest U.S. aviation disaster, officials said.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, JENNIFER PELTZ and TED SHAFFREY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Weapons, ammo stopped from heading into Mexico
item.description: A cache of weapons and hundreds of rounds of ammunition have been seized from vehicles recently at the Del Rio Port of Entry, U.S. Customs and Border Protection says.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: House passes budget framework
item.description: President Donald Trump's agenda is now one step closer to getting funding from Congress because the House passed a budget framework.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Brandt, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court rules Trump administration must 'facilitate’ return of mistakenly deported man
item.description: The court’s emergency ruling instructs the lower judge to clarify the language of her order but said she acted properly in ensuring that Kilmar Abrego Garcia’s case is handled “as it would have been had he not been improperly sent to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: The Latest: Supreme Court tells Trump administration to facilitate return of mistakenly deported man
item.description: The Supreme Court on Thursday said President Donald Trump’s administration must facilitate the return of Kilmar Abrego Garcia, a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported to El Salvador, rejecting the administration’s emergency appeal.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gambian torture victims testify in US against a member of former dictator's military
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Suspected of backing a coup plot against the longtime dictator of Gambia nearly 20 years ago, Pharing Sanyang described Thursday how he was beaten with pipes and palm tree branches, pistol-whipped and hit in the face with a hammer.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hip-hop exec, accused LA gang leader pleads not guilty to racketeering, extortion charges
item.description: A music executive who authorities allege is a leader of a notorious Los Angeles street gang appeared in court Tuesday to face a wide range of charges, including extortion, racketeering and conspiracy.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, WGN 9

item.title: Trump's tariffs threaten to end quarter-century era of cheap goods for U.S. consumers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s new tariffs threaten to push up prices on clothes, mobile phones, furniture and many other products in the coming months, possibly ending the era of cheap goods that Americans enjoyed for about a quarter-century before the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER RUGABER and ANNE D'INNOCENZIO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Republican bill requiring proof of citizenship for voting passes US House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House passed one of Republicans' signature issues for the year on Thursday, approving legislation to require proof of U.S. citizenship when registering to vote for federal elections, one of President Donald Trump's top election-related priorities.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, CHRISTINE FERNANDO and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: House Republicans pass bill requiring proof of citizenship to vote
item.description: House Republicans for a second time passed a stand-alone bill that would require proof of citizenship to vote in federal elections and impose voter roll purge requirements on states. The legislation — formally titled the Safeguard American Voter Eligibility (SAVE) Act — passed in a 220-208 vote.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, ABC 4

item.title: UTA announces 'significant' changes to several bus services including new routes
item.description: The Utah Transit Authority said significant changes are coming to its services in Davis, Salt Lake, and Utah County.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: Jury awards $1.68 billion to women who accused writer and director James Toback of sexual abuse
item.description: The judgement marks one of the largest jury awards since the advent of the #MeToo movement, as well as in New York state history
item.dc:creator: Susan Haigh, Fox 5

item.title: No new hires, unless you prove AI can’t do the job: Shopify CEO
item.description: "Teams must demonstrate why they cannot get what they want done using AI," Shopify CEO Tobi Lutke wrote in a memo to employees.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kooiman, Fox 5
